actor_relation_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee of the Penford board
Perkins Coie LLP (referred to as Perkins Coie), Penford’s legal counsel
retain Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor
Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board
Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board

event_claims.description: Mr. Hatfield decided to utilize the Executive Committee to discuss recent developments because it could meet with little advance notice and had authority under the Company's bylaws.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Hatfield decided to utilize the Executive Committee to discuss recent developments, due to the Executive Committee’s ability to meet with little advance notice and the authority vested in this committee under the Company’s bylaws

event_claims.actor_label: Executive Committee of the Penford board
event_claims.description: Notice was provided for a meeting of the Executive Committee to discuss Schedule 13D filings and strategic developments, including discussions with Ingredion.
event_claims.quote_text: on July 22, 2014, Mr. Lawlor, as secretary of the Company, provided notice of a meeting of the Executive Committee of the Penford board for the purpose of discussing, among other things, recent Schedule 13D filings, including the SEACOR Filing and strategic developments, including the discussions with Ingredion

event_claims.actor_label: Executive Committee of the Penford board
event_claims.description: The Executive Committee held a telephonic meeting attended by committee members, other directors, management, and Perkins Coie representatives.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2014, the Executive Committee of the Penford board held a telephonic meeting, at which all members of the Executive Committee, as well as certain other directors, members of Penford’s management and representatives of Perkins Coie LLP (referred to as Perkins Coie), Penford’s legal counsel, were present

event_claims.actor_label: Executive Committee of the Penford board
event_claims.description: The Executive Committee directed management to retain Deutsche Bank as financial advisor to advise on shareholder matters and strategic alternatives.
event_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee directed management to proceed to retain Deutsche Bank as the Company’s financial advisor and to advise on shareholder matters and to evaluate strategic alternatives, and directed management to negotiate a suitable engagement letter

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee met with management, Perkins Coie, and Deutsche Bank to discuss Ingredion's indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 11, 2014, the Executive Committee met with members of management and representatives of each of Perkins Coie and Deutsche Bank, at which meeting they discussed Ingredion’s indication of interest received on August 10

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee unanimously determined to proceed with further review and investigation of Ingredion's indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: the Executive Committee unanimously determined that it would be in the best interests of the Company and its shareholders to proceed with further review and investigation of Ingredion’s indication of interest to acquire Penford

event_claims.description: Because SEACOR was identified as a potential counterparty, the Executive Committee determined Mr. Hatfield should contact Mr. Behrens to discuss whether SEACOR had acquisition interest.
event_claims.quote_text: since SEACOR had been identified by Penford’s financial advisor as a potential counterparty, the Executive Committee determined that Mr. Hatfield should promptly contact Mr. Behrens, the SEACOR representative on the board, to discuss whether SEACOR had an interest in acquiring the Company

event_claims.description: The Executive Committee indicated Mr. Behrens should recuse himself from future board sale-process discussions if SEACOR had acquisition interest because of a potential conflict between duties to shareholders and duties to SEACOR.
event_claims.quote_text: The Executive Committee indicated that if SEACOR did have such an interest, Mr. Behrens should recuse himself from future board discussions related to a possible sale process due to the potential conflict between Mr. Behrens’ duties to the Company’s shareholders and his duties to SEACOR

event_claims.description: Mr. Behrens stated SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 12, 2014, Mr. Hatfield discussed the Ingredion indication of interest with Mr. Behrens to determine if SEACOR was interested in acquiring the Company. Mr. Behrens stated that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time

event_claims.description: Before substantive board discussions, Mr. Hatfield asked Mr. Behrens to confirm SEACOR's position regarding a potential transaction with Penford.
event_claims.quote_text: Prior to engaging in substantive discussions, Mr. Hatfield requested that Mr. Behrens confirm the position of SEACOR with respect to a potential transaction with Penford

event_claims.description: Mr. Behrens confirmed SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Behrens confirmed that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time

event_claims.description: The board unanimously directed management to proceed with further investigation and development of Ingredion's proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: Following discussion among the board members, the board members present at the meeting unanimously directed management to proceed with further investigation and development of the proposal from Ingredion

event_claims.description: The board authorized management to engage with Ingredion and provide additional information regarding the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: the board authorized management to engage with Ingredion and provide additional information regarding the Company

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process described at the meeting.
event_claims.quote_text: The board also authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with the market check process as described at the meeting

event_claims.description: Perkins Coie reviewed and led a discussion of the Penford board's fiduciary duties, including in connection with change-in-control strategic proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: Perkins Coie provided a review, and led a discussion, of the fiduciary duties of Penford’s board, including in connection with strategic proposals involving a change in control of Penford
